sections.paragraph:
θεομακαρίτης, ου, ὁ (hapax leg.; the noun μακαρίτης =‘one blessed’, i.e. ‘dead, lately dead’ [Aeschyl. et al.]). As adj. (like μακαρίτης Aristoph., Pl. 555) divinely blessed of Christ’s passion ISm 1:2 v.l. (s. θεομακάριστος).—DELG s.v. μάκαρ.

hapax hapax leg. = hapax legomenon (occurring only once)

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

adj. adj. = adjective

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 449
